caption: Confocal microscopy to examine rhBMP-7 binding to cell surface HSPGs in the absence or presence of heparin and DS. Cells were incubated for 1 h at 4 °C with purified rhBMP-7 (10 μg/mL) alone or in the presence of heparin (0.1 g/L) or DS (0.5 g/L). Cells pre-treated with heparinase I/III (indicated a heparinase) and cells washed with acidic buffer (indicated as acid buffer) were also incubated for 1 h at 4 °C with purified rhBMP-7 alone. rhBMP-7 was stained red, while HSPGs were stained green. Nuclear counterstaining (blue) was carried out using DAPI. The merge image highlights colocalization (yellow) of rhBMP-7 and HSPGs. Scale bar, 5 μm.
answers: A1: The imaging modality represented in this image is confocal microscopy.
A2: The image does not depict a body region or anatomical area. Instead, it represents a cellular level study, specifically the binding behavior of rhBMP-7 to cell surface HSPGs.
A3: The caption does not report any abnormalities identified in the image.
A4: Based on the caption, the image doesn't show any regularities or irregularities. It provides a detailed depiction of a cellular process.

A5: The image does contain noteworthy labels or indexes. Specifically, these are the different staining colors used: rhBMP-7 is stained red, HSPGs are stained green, and nuclear counterstaining is carried out using DAPI (blue). There's also an indication of colocalization (yellow) of rhBMP-7 and HSPGs. The scale bar is another important index - it indicates that the scale of the image is 5 μm.

caption: Cortical regions within PSC where BOLD responses to reward were affected by central dopamine level.In those trials in which either the left or right index finger (IF) had been rewarded for correct judgment, regions within contralateral PSC (including BA1, BA2, and BA3b) showed changes in BOLD responses reflecting an interaction between central dopamine levels and reward, at the time point corresponding to when financial reward or nonreward was delivered visually (drug-by-reward interaction thresholded at p = 0.05, FWE-corrected, see Materials and Methods for further details; LH, left hemisphere; RH, right hemisphere). Reactivation of contralateral PSC by visual reward-delivery (Table S2b; peaking at −34, −40, 60 for left PSC; and 36, −30, 50 for right PSC) increased with increased central dopamine levels; an effect comparable to the drug effect seen in ventral striatum and OFC (cf. Figure 3).
answers: A1: The imaging modality is not explicitly stated in the caption, however, given that it references BOLD responses, the imaging modality is most likely functional Magnetic Resonance Imaging (fMRI).
A2: This image depicts the cortical regions within the Posterior Superior Cortex (PSC), specifically Brodmann areas 1, 2, and 3b (BA1, BA2, and BA3b) in the brain.

A3: The caption does not indicate any abnormalities. It mentions changes in BOLD responses due to an interaction between dopamine levels and reward, but this is not implied to be an abnormal condition.
A4: Based on the description, the image appears normal as it shows expected changes in BOLD responses in response to reward stimuli and dopamine levels.

A5: The image contains significant information about locations within the left and right PSC reacting to rewards. Noteworthy coordinates are given, peaking at −34, −40, 60 for the left PSC, and 36, −30, 50 for the right PSC. This notably shows the effect of increased dopamine levels on these regions.
